restore_note: This section's cases were accidentally deleted from TestRail (section tree intact, 0 cases live). No original Suite 6 source_case_ids were recorded when this was first migrated.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that submitting the signup form without an email shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Sign Up page.

steps.content: Enter a website URL and password, leaving the email field empty.
steps.expected: Values should be entered in website and password fields.

steps.content: Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox.
Click "Get Started".
steps.expected: "Email is required" error message should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that submitting signup with an already-registered email shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Sign Up page. An existing account is registered with a known email address.

steps.content: Enter the existing account's email address in the email field.
Enter a valid website URL and password.
Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox.
Click "Get Started".
steps.expected: "The email has already been taken." error message should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that submitting signup with an invalid email format shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Sign Up page.

steps.content: Enter an invalid email address (e.g., "notanemail") in the email field.
Enter a valid website URL and password.
Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox.
Click "Get Started".
steps.expected: "Valid email required" error message should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change. Verified live: unlike the Login page (where an invalid email format is blocked by the browser's native tooltip with no app message), this form's own JS validation catches it and shows this exact custom message, even though the field is also `type="email"`.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that submitting signup with an email longer than 190 characters shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Sign Up page.

steps.content: Enter an email address exceeding 190 characters in the email field.
Enter a valid website URL and password.
Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox.
Click "Get Started".
steps.expected: "Your email address exceeds the maximum value of 190 characters" error message should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change. No run_type set in the original publish. Verified live with a 194-character email (field accepts full length, no client-side truncation).

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that submitting signup with an invalid website URL shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Sign Up page.

steps.content: Enter a valid email address and password.
Enter an invalid website URL (not in "example.com" format) in the website field.
Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox.
Click "Get Started".
steps.expected: "Please enter URL in the format example.com" error message should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that submitting signup with a website URL longer than 75 characters shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Sign Up page.

steps.content: Enter a valid email address and password.

Enter a website URL longer than 75 characters in the website field, using multiple valid domain labels (e.g. "sub1.sub2.sub3....example.com") rather than one long label — a single DNS label over ~63 characters fails the URL-format check first and never reaches the length check.
Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox.
Click "Get Started".
steps.expected: "URL should be less than 75 characters" error message should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change. No run_type set in the original publish. Verified live: a single 76-char label (e.g. "aaa...a.com") triggers "Please enter URL in the format example.com" instead — the format regex rejects the label before length is even checked. An 80-char multi-label domain (each label short) correctly triggers this case's message.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that submitting signup with a password shorter than 8 characters shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Sign Up page.

steps.content: Enter a valid email address and website URL.
Enter a password with fewer than 8 characters in the password field.
Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox.
Click "Get Started".
steps.expected: "The password must be at least 8 characters long" error message should be displayed below the password field.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change. Mismatch found: expected "Minimum 8 characters required" | actual (verified live) "The password must be at least 8 characters long" — same wording as the Forgot Password > Reset Password page's short-password message, confirming this is a shared, app-wide validation string.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that clicking "Verify Email" in the signup confirmation email opens the email verification success page
cases.preconditions: User has completed signup with a new email address. The inbox contains a "Verify your email for CookieYes" email.

steps.content: Open the "Verify your email for CookieYes" email in the inbox.
steps.expected: Verification email should be present and opened.

steps.content: Click the "Verify Email" button in the email.
steps.expected: The link is `/user-verify?token=<token>&identity=<identity>`, redirecting to `/v1/user-verify?...`. The email verification success page should be displayed: a success icon, the heading "Your email is verified!", the message "You have successfully verified your email address.", and a "Go to Dashboard" button.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change. Verified live via a real signup + real confirmation email (link provided out-of-band by the user).

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that clicking "Go to Dashboard" after email verification navigates to the Dashboard
cases.preconditions: User has clicked "Verify Email" and is on the email verification success page.

steps.content: Click "Go to Dashboard".

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change. Verified live end-to-end via a real signup + confirmation email.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that clicking an expired verification email link shows a failure page with a resend option
cases.preconditions: User has signed up but has not verified their email within 1 hour. The original "Verify your email for CookieYes" email is still in the inbox.

steps.content: Open the "Verify your email for CookieYes" email (more than 1 hour after signup).
steps.expected: Email should be accessible in the inbox.

steps.content: Click "Verify Email".
steps.expected: "Your email verification failed!" page should be displayed.

steps.content: Click "Resend Verification Email".
steps.expected: A new verification email should be received and a "Verification email has been resent successfully." notification should appear.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change. Not automatable this pass: requires waiting 1+ hour on a real signup's verification token, plus real inbox access for both the original and resent email. Strong adjacent evidence gathered live instead: a fresh, never-verified signup hit with a garbage token+identity (never a real expiry) produced exactly this failure page (red X icon, "Your email verification failed!", "Please try again or click the button below to receive a new verification email.", "Resend Verification Email" button), and clicking Resend showed the notification "Verification email has been resent successfully." character-for-character matching this case's expected text. A cooldown timer ("Resend email in N seconds") also appeared, undocumented elsewhere — see the new suggested case. Root cause (real 1-hour expiry) still unconfirmed, but the failure-page shape and resend behavior are now verified via the closest obtainable proxy.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that clicking a verification link after account deletion shows a failure page
cases.preconditions: User completed signup but deleted their account before verifying email. The original "Verify your email for CookieYes" email is still in the inbox.

steps.content: Open the "Verify your email for CookieYes" email from the inbox.
Click "Verify Email".
steps.expected: "Your email verification failed!" page should be displayed with a "Resend Verification Email" button.

steps.content: Click "Resend Verification Email".
steps.expected: A new verification email should be received and a success notification should appear. [Note: source cases C70 and C1148 differ on this expected result — verify manually before automation.]

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-signup-core.json) — no content change. No run_type set in the original publish. Source cases C70/C1148 disagreed on this expected result even before restoration — flagged in the original case. Not automatable this pass: requires a real signup, real inbox access, and deleting the account before verifying — a destructive precondition combined with email-required. Same adjacent evidence as the expired-token case applies here too: a garbage-token verify attempt reproduces the identical failure page and confirms the Resend button's success notification is exactly "Verification email has been resent successfully." — this resolves the C70/C1148 disagreement in favor of that exact wording, though the account-deletion precondition specifically remains unconfirmed.

cases.title: [Sign Up] Verify that clicking "Resend Verification Email" on the failure page starts a cooldown timer before it can be clicked again
cases.preconditions:
A verification link has been invalidated for testing purposes:
1. Take a verification link in the format `/user-verify?token=<token>&identity=<identity>`
2. Replace the `token` and `identity` values with arbitrary invalid strings (the `identity` value must be invalid — a link with a real, already-verified `identity` will succeed even if the `token` is garbage)
3. Navigate to the edited link

User is on the resulting "Your email verification failed!" page, which shows a "Resend Verification Email" button.

steps.content: Click "Resend Verification Email".
steps.expected: A "Verification email has been resent successfully." notification should appear.

steps.content: Observe the resend control immediately after the notification appears.
steps.expected: The "Resend Verification Email" button should no longer be present. In its place, the page should display "Resend email in <N> seconds" (observed starting between 56–58 seconds across separate checks).

steps.content: Wait several seconds without reloading the page.
steps.expected: The seconds count in "Resend email in <N> seconds" should visibly decrease over time (observed 58 → 43 seconds), confirming it is a live countdown and not a static message.

cases.rewrite_notes: New case, no Suite 6 precedent — gap first identified 2026-07-21 during the Sign Up Core grill, drafted then but dropped unapproved when the review gate closed (see coverage-gaps.md). Re-verified live 2026-07-23: garbling both token and identity on a /user-verify link reliably reproduces the failure page without needing a real signup, a 1-hour wait, or inbox access — this is now folded into the precondition as the repro method. Clicking Resend replaces the button with countdown text (the button element is absent entirely, not merely disabled) and shows the same resend-success toast as sibling case 39710. Countdown observed ticking from 58s to 43s, confirming it's live. NOT verified this pass: whether the "Resend Verification Email" button reappears once the countdown reaches 0 — did not wait out the full duration. Distinct from case 37242 in cases-my-account.json, which covers an analogous cooldown on My Account's email-update flow, not signup verification. This case's precondition uses a manufactured invalid link (not a real 1-hour wait) because the cooldown behavior under test is triggered by the Resend click itself, not by the reason the page is showing a failure — same assumption 39710 already relies on for the page's copy/toast text. The unconfirmed real-expiry root cause remains 39710's open gap, not reintroduced here.
